Defines the ExpandableDialog structure and functions for creating, configuring, and managing expandable dialogs with scrollable text and action bars.

# Purpose
The code defines an `ExpandableDialog` structure and related functions for creating and managing expandable dialog windows in a graphical user interface. The `ExpandableDialog` is a specialized dialog that can display a large amount of text with scrolling capabilities. It includes an action bar that indicates available scrolling directions and can optionally include a selectable button action. The structure contains various components such as a `Dialog`, `ActionBarLayer`, `ScrollLayer`, and `TextLayer`, along with properties for managing icons, fonts, and colors.

The file provides a set of functions to create, initialize, and manipulate `ExpandableDialog` instances. These functions include [`expandable_dialog_create`](<#expandable_dialog_create>) and [`expandable_dialog_create_with_params`](<#expandable_dialog_create_with_params>) for creating dialogs, [`expandable_dialog_init`](<#expandable_dialog_init>) for initialization, and several setter functions to configure the dialog's appearance and behavior, such as [`expandable_dialog_set_header`](<#expandable_dialog_set_header>) and [`expandable_dialog_set_action_bar_background_color`](<#expandable_dialog_set_action_bar_background_color>). Additionally, the code includes functions to manage the dialog's position in the window stack, such as [`expandable_dialog_push`](<#expandable_dialog_push>) and [`expandable_dialog_pop`](<#expandable_dialog_pop>). The file serves as a part of a user interface library, providing a public API for developers to integrate expandable dialogs into their applications.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`
- `applib/ui/click.h`
- `applib/ui/action_bar_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.h`
- `applib/ui/scroll_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/window_stack.h`
- `resource/resource_ids.auto.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Data Structures

---
### ExpandableDialog
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``dialog``: Contains the base dialog structure.
    - ``show_action_bar``: Indicates if the action bar is visible.
    - ``show_action_icon_animated``: Indicates if the action icons are animated.
    - ``action_bar_background_color``: Specifies the background color of the action bar.
    - ``action_bar``: Holds the action bar layer.
    - ``select_click_handler``: Defines the click handler for the select button.
    - ``up_icon``: Points to the bitmap for the up icon.
    - ``select_icon``: Points to the bitmap for the select icon.
    - ``down_icon``: Points to the bitmap for the down icon.
    - ``header_font``: Specifies the font used for the header text.
    - ``header``: Stores the header text with a maximum length defined by `DIALOG_MAX_HEADER_LEN`.
    - ``header_layer``: Contains the text layer for the header.
    - ``scroll_layer``: Holds the scroll layer for the dialog content.
    - ``content_down_arrow_layer``: Contains the layer for the down arrow in the content.
- **Description**: Manages a dialog that can display a large amount of text with scrolling capabilities and an optional action bar for navigation and selection actions.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### expandable\_dialog\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:expandable_dialog_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/expandable_dialog.h#L84>)

Initializes an ExpandableDialog with a specified name.
- **Description**: Use this function to prepare an `ExpandableDialog` for use by setting its initial state and associating it with a given name. This function must be called before using the dialog to ensure it is properly configured. It sets up the dialog's window handlers and initializes the action bar to be visible by default. Ensure that the `expandable_dialog` pointer is valid and not null before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `expandable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ExpandableDialog` structure that will be initialized. Must not be null.
    - `dialog_name`: String representing the name to assign to the dialog. The caller retains ownership of this string.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`expandable_dialog_init`](<expandable_dialog.c.md#expandable_dialog_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### expandable\_dialog\_set\_action\_icon\_animated<!-- {{#callable_declaration:expandable_dialog_set_action_icon_animated}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/expandable_dialog.h#L102>)

Sets the animation state of the action icon in an expandable dialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify whether the action icon in an expandable dialog should animate. This function is useful when you want to provide visual feedback to users through animation. It is important to note that this function will not have any visible effect unless the action bar is shown using `expandable_dialog_show_action_bar` with a true value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `expandable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ExpandableDialog` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the `show_action_icon_animated` field of this structure.
    - `animated`: Boolean value indicating whether the action icon should animate. Use true to enable animation and false to disable it.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`expandable_dialog_set_action_icon_animated`](<expandable_dialog.c.md#expandable_dialog_set_action_icon_animated>)  (Implementation)


---
### expandable\_dialog\_set\_action\_bar\_background\_color<!-- {{#callable_declaration:expandable_dialog_set_action_bar_background_color}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/expandable_dialog.h#L108>)

Sets the background color of the action bar in an expandable dialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the background color of the action bar in an `ExpandableDialog`. This function is useful when you want to customize the appearance of the dialog's action bar to match a specific theme or design. Ensure that the `ExpandableDialog` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `expandable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ExpandableDialog` for which to set the action bar background color. Must not be null.
    - `background_color`: The `GColor` value representing the desired background color for the action bar.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`expandable_dialog_set_action_bar_background_color`](<expandable_dialog.c.md#expandable_dialog_set_action_bar_background_color>)  (Implementation)


---
### expandable\_dialog\_set\_header<!-- {{#callable_declaration:expandable_dialog_set_header}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/expandable_dialog.h#L117>)

Sets the header text of an expandable dialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to set or update the header text of an expandable dialog. The header text is optional and can be set to null to remove it. The text has a maximum length defined by `DIALOG_MAX_HEADER_LEN`, and any text exceeding this length will be clipped. This function must be called with a valid `ExpandableDialog` pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `expandable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ExpandableDialog` on which to set the header. Must not be null.
    - `header`: Text to set as the header. Can be null to remove the header. If not null, the text will be clipped to `DIALOG_MAX_HEADER_LEN` characters.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`expandable_dialog_set_header`](<expandable_dialog.c.md#expandable_dialog_set_header>)  (Implementation)


---
### expandable\_dialog\_set\_header\_font<!-- {{#callable_declaration:expandable_dialog_set_header_font}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/expandable_dialog.h#L122>)

Sets the font for the header text of an expandable dialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify the font for the header text in an expandable dialog. This function must be called after the expandable dialog is initialized. It does not validate the font, so ensure that the `header_font` is a valid `GFont` object before calling this function. The function directly assigns the font to the dialog's header, affecting how the header text is displayed.
- **Inputs**:
    - `expandable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ExpandableDialog` on which to set the header font. Must not be null.
    - `header_font`: The `GFont` to use for the header text. Must be a valid font object.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`expandable_dialog_set_header_font`](<expandable_dialog.c.md#expandable_dialog_set_header_font>)  (Implementation)
